title: Wrong number of arguments in function call
explanation: This error occurs when you call a function with a different number of arguments than its definition requires. Unlike some languages, Rust does not support optional arguments or function overloading.
error_message: error[E0061]: this function takes 2 arguments but 1 argument was supplied

fixes.description: Provide the correct number of arguments
Use a builder pattern for many optional parameters

tip: Rust does not have default arguments or overloading. Use Option<T>, the builder pattern, or different function names for variants.
